behavior_summary: When the player examines or 'looks at' this object, the script displays a short descriptive line: "torture chair".
After showing the line it suspends/ends the U8P_TORTCHAR::look process with no further interaction, branching, or state changes.
This is a single-purpose U8P_TORTCHAR::look/description handler (process type 0x0208) intended purely to provide a short in-world label or description.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TORTCHAR::look: present a single Bark node containing the text "torture chair".
Immediately after the Bark the process issues a suspend/terminate action (inferred from an Unknown/suspend node), so execution ends with no branching.
There are no menu presents, conditionals, calls to other scripts, or global flag reads/writes in this routine.
open_questions: Is this U8P_TORTCHAR::look handler only invoked by an explicit player 'U8P_TORTCHAR::look/examine' command, or also by entering a proximity/fast-area trigger? (inferred from processType 0x0208 but not explicit).
Does the suspend node simply end the U8P_TORTCHAR::look process or hand control back for other engine post-processing (exact suspend semantics are engine-defined and not present in the script)?
Are there any animation/sound ties to this object elsewhere in the game data that accompany the textual bark? (no references in this script)

main_function.name: U8P_TORTCHAR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive text shown to the player when they examine or 'U8P_TORTCHAR::look at' the object (display "torture chair"), then end/suspend the U8P_TORTCHAR::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by a U8P_TORTCHAR::look/examine action (engine U8P_TORTCHAR::look-type process, processType 0x0208).
Likely triggered when the player inspects the object or enters the object's U8P_TORTCHAR::look-trigger region (inferred from classification).
